mission.title: Install and Configure OpenChoreo
mission.description: OpenChoreo is a CNCF open-source internal developer platform for Kubernetes providing higher-level abstractions, a Backstage-powered developer portal, integrated CI/CD, GitOps workflows, and observability.

steps.title: Add the OpenChoreo Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official OpenChoreo Helm chart repository.

```bash
helm repo add openchoreo https://charts.openchoreo.dev
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install OpenChoreo using Helm
steps.description:
Install OpenChoreo into a dedicated namespace.

```bash
helm install openchoreo openchoreo/openchoreo --namespace openchoreo-system --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify OpenChoreo pods are running
steps.description:
Check that all OpenChoreo components are running.

```bash
kubectl get pods -n openchoreo-system
```

> You should see the control plane, data plane, and portal pods in Running state.

steps.title: Access the developer portal
steps.description:
Port-forward to access the Backstage-powered developer portal.

```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/openchoreo-portal -n openchoreo-system 3000:3000
```

> Open http://localhost:3000 to access the developer portal.

uninstall.title: Uninstall OpenChoreo
uninstall.description:
Remove OpenChoreo from your cluster.

```bash
helm uninstall openchoreo -n openchoreo-system
```
